factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We plan to spend between $700 million and $800 million in 2015 as we continue with product launches and facility improvements. Approximately 50 percent of our capital expenditures are expected to be invested outside of the U.S. in 2015.

factors.summary: Aftermarket service revenue is growing, contributing to overall sales.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the six months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Fuel systems sales increased due to the new Beijing Foton ISG engine that entered production in the second quarter of 2014 in China and increased aftermarket demand.

factors.summary: Dealer inventory levels and orders reflect underlying demand trends, which vary by region.

evidence.text: Distribution segment sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014, primarily due to $315 million of segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, $11 million of segment sales related to the acquisition of international distributors and $36 million of organic sales growth primarily in Africa and Eastern Europe, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Australia, Canada and Europe) and decreased sales in Asia Pacific, China, Western Europe and Russia.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments due to growth in the Distribution segment.

evidence.text:
•We expect continued growth in the North American heavy-duty and medium-duty on-highway markets compared to 2014.
•We expect North American light-duty demand to remain strong.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are contributing to improved margins through lower research and development expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in net income and earnings per share was driven by improved gross margin and lower research, development and engineering expenses, partially offset by higher selling, general and administrative expenses and lower equity, royalty and interest income from investees.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly tightening emissions controls, are creating opportunities for the company.

evidence.text: •tightening emissions controls across the world;

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is a challenge due to varying adoption and enforcement of emission standards.

evidence.text: •unpredictability in the adoption, implementation and enforcement of emission standards around the world;

factors.summary: Management has provided forward guidance expecting continued growth in North American markets but cautioning about international challenges.

evidence.text:
•We expect continued growth in the North American heavy-duty and medium-duty on-highway markets compared to 2014.
•We expect North American light-duty demand to remain strong.

factors.summary: Management is confident with a long-term strategy focused on macroeconomic trends and is executing on strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: We believe that, over the longer term, there will be economic improvements in most of our current markets and that our opportunities for long-term profitable growth will continue as the result of the following four macroeconomic trends that should benefit our businesses:

factors.summary: Near-term risks include weak demand in international markets, foreign currency volatility, and challenges in certain end markets.

evidence.text:
•Power generation markets are expected to remain weak.
•Weak economic conditions in Brazil will continue to negatively impact demand across our businesses.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on four macroeconomic trends: tightening emissions, infrastructure needs, energy availability, and globalization.

evidence.text: We believe that, over the longer term, there will be economic improvements in most of our current markets and that our opportunities for long-term profitable growth will continue as the result of the following four macroeconomic trends that should benefit our businesses:

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and planned distributor acquisitions which are expected to boost sales.

evidence.text:
•We plan to acquire two more unconsolidated North American distributors in the third quarter, which we expect to increase our Distribution segment sales.
•We expect the new ISG engine, which began production in the second quarter of 2014 with our Beijing Foton Cummins Engine Co., Ltd. joint venture, to continue to gain market share in China in its first full year of production.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share in China with the new ISG engine.

evidence.text: •We expect the new ISG engine, which began production in the second quarter of 2014 with our Beijing Foton Cummins Engine Co., Ltd. joint venture, to continue to gain market share in China in its first full year of production.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are creating both opportunities and challenges for the company.

evidence.text:
•tightening emissions controls across the world;
•increasingly stringent environmental laws and regulations;

factors.summary: Input costs have been favorable, with lower material and commodity costs contributing to improved margins. However, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations partially offset these positive effects.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to improved Distribution segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, higher volumes and lower material and commodity costs, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Australia, Brazil and Canada).

The increase in gross margin was primarily due to improved Distribution segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, higher volumes and lower material and commodity costs, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

factors.summary: The company faces significant foreign exchange exposure, primarily in Brazil, Europe, and other regions, which has unfavorably impacted its sales and earnings. This exposure is a notable risk, as seen in the unfavorable fluctuations affecting both sales and EBIT, particularly in Europe and Brazil.

evidence.text: Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results (primarily in Brazil and Europe).

Components segment EBIT for the three and six months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable periods in 2014, primarily due to higher gross margin, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Europe and Brazil) and higher research, development and engineering expenses.
Unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations which impacted sales by approximately 4 percent (primarily in Europe, Brazil, Australia, Canada and the U.K.).

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed with expanding gross and operating margins due to lower costs and higher volumes, but segment EBIT margins are under pressure from foreign currency fluctuations and cost increases.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased for the three months ended June 28, 2015, versus the comparable period in 2014, and increased as a percentage of sales by 1.7 percentage points. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to improved Distribution segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, higher volumes and lower material and commodity costs, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

Distribution segment EBIT as a percentage of sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, was 7.6 percent compared to 10.2 percent for the comparable period in 2014. The decrease was due to the dilutive effect of the 2014 acquisitions and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with growth observed in certain segments such as heavy-duty and medium-duty truck engines, as well as in North American markets, while declines are noted in industrial, mining, and international sectors.

evidence.text: Unit shipments by engine classification (including unit shipments to Power Generation) were as follows: Mid-range: 120,000 vs. 118,700 (+1%), Heavy-duty: 32,800 vs. 30,300 (+8%), High-horsepower: 3,700 vs. 3,900 (-5%).

Components segment sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Emission solutions sales increased primarily due to improved demand in the North American on-highway markets, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Europe and Brazil).
•We expect continued growth in the North American heavy-duty and medium-duty on-highway markets compared to 2014. •We expect North American light-duty demand to remain strong.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are mixed, with growth in North America and certain end markets, but challenges persist in emerging markets like Brazil.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to improved Distribution segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, higher volumes and lower material and commodity costs, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Australia, Brazil and Canada).
•We expect continued growth in the North American heavy-duty and medium-duty on-highway markets compared to 2014.
•Weak economic conditions in Brazil will continue to negatively impact demand across our businesses.

factors.summary: The company is enhancing shareholder returns through a combination of share repurchases and increased dividends. The evidence indicates a positive approach to returning value to shareholders with specific actions taken in 2015.

evidence.text: We may continue to repurchase outstanding shares from time to time during 2015 to offset the dilutive impact of employee stock based compensation plans and to enhance shareholder value.
In July 2015, the Board of Directors authorized a dividend increase of 25 percent from $0.78 per share to $0.975 per share on a quarterly basis.
We paid dividends of $280 million during the six months ended June 28, 2015.
During the first six months of 2015, we repurchased $174 million of common stock under the 2012 Board of Directors Authorized Plan, completing this program in the second quarter of 2015.

factors.summary: The company is actively pursuing a multi-year acquisition strategy focused on North American distributors, with planned expenditures ranging from $150 million to $190 million. This strategic move aims to enhance distribution capabilities and boost sales within the distribution segment.

evidence.text: In September 2013, we announced our intention to acquire the equity that we do not already own in most of our partially-owned U.S. and Canadian distributors over a three to five year period.
We plan to spend $150 million to $190 million on North American distributor acquisitions and the related debt retirements in the third quarter of 2015.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong free cash flow generation, which is stable and supports its operations and growth initiatives. The evidence shows consistent cash generation, with $569 million generated in the first half of 2015, indicating a positive financial position.

evidence.text: Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong. Our solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable ready access to credit and the capital markets. We assess our liquidity in terms of our ability to generate adequate cash to fund our operating, investing and financing activities. We believe our liquidity provides us with the financial flexibility needed to fund working capital, capital expenditures, common stock repurchases, dividend payments, acquisitions of our North American distributors, projected pension obligations and debt service obligations. We continue to generate cash from operations in the U.S. and maintain access to $1.7 billion of our revolving credit facility.
We generated $569 million of operating cash flows for the six months ended June 28, 2015, compared to $701 million for the same period in 2014.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable, with a consistent debt to capital ratio of around 17%, indicating manageable debt levels. The maintained credit ratings further support the stability of the company's debt leverage.

evidence.text: Our ratings and outlook from each of the credit rating agencies as of the date of filing are shown in the table below. Credit ratings are not recommendations to buy, are subject to change and each rating should be evaluated independently of any other rating. In addition, we undertake no obligation to update disclosures concerning our credit ratings, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.
Our debt to capital ratio (total capital defined as debt plus equity) at June 28, 2015 , was 17.0 percent , compared to 17.3 percent at December 31, 2014 .

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with strong performance in North American on-highway markets and emission solutions, but facing challenges in international markets such as Brazil and China due to weak economic conditions and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Emission solutions sales increased primarily due to improved demand in the North American on-highway markets, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Europe and Brazil).
•Power generation markets are expected to remain weak. •Weak economic conditions in Brazil will continue to negatively impact demand across our businesses.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed organic growth performance across different regions. While there is notable growth in North American markets and specific regions like Africa and Eastern Europe, other areas such as Asia Pacific, China, Western Europe, and Russia face challenges, including unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations and decreased sales.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth is present in certain regions and products, but faces challenges in others.

Distribution segment sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014, primarily due to ... $36 million of organic sales growth primarily in Africa and Eastern Europe, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations ... and decreased sales in Asia Pacific, China, Western Europe and Russia.
We expect continued growth in the North American heavy-duty and medium-duty on-highway markets compared to 2014.
We expect North American light-duty demand to remain strong.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments and regions, with growth observed in North American markets and certain international areas, but challenges remain in others, particularly in Asia Pacific, China, Western Europe, and Russia.

evidence.text: Demand trends vary by segment, with some businesses experiencing growth while others face challenges.

Distribution segment sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014, primarily due to $315 million of segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2013, $11 million of segment sales related to the acquisition of international distributors and $36 million of organic sales growth primarily in Africa and Eastern Europe, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Australia, Canada and Europe) and decreased sales in Asia Pacific, China, Western Europe and Russia.
We expect continued growth in the North American heavy-duty and medium-duty on-highway markets compared to 2014.
We expect North American light-duty demand to remain strong.

factors.summary: The company faces significant sensitivities including foreign currency fluctuations, geopolitical risks, economic conditions in emerging markets, and commodity price volatility. These factors could negatively impact the company's sales, earnings, and overall financial performance.

evidence.text: Components segment EBIT for the three and six months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable periods in 2014, primarily due to higher gross margin, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Europe and Brazil) and higher research, development and engineering expenses.
Foreign currency volatility could continue to put pressure on sales and earnings.
Domestic and international mining markets could continue to deteriorate if commodity prices continue to weaken.

factors.summary: Construction and infrastructure spending presents mixed signals, with increased demand in North America but declines in international markets.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended June 28, 2015, increased versus the comparable period in 2014. The following were the primary drivers by business: •Emission solutions sales increased primarily due to improved demand in the North American on-highway markets, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in Europe and Brazil).
•Declines in international construction and commercial marine demand.
